text: In the LapSim VAS module, the surgeon uses endosteplars to sequentially divide the vessels, fissures, and bronchus during the dissection and mobilization of the upper right lung lobe for complete lobe resection. Sponge sticks or other selected instruments are used to prepare the structures.

Thoracoscopic forceps are introduced through the posterior port, passing behind the superior pulmonary vein after identifying the middle lobe vein. The superior pulmonary vein is encircled with a vessel rubber loop, creating space for a stapler. An endovascular stapler is introduced through the posterior port, and its thin blade is passed behind the superior pulmonary vein, which is then divided after the removal of the vessel loop.

After dividing the pulmonary vein, the surgeon visualizes the pulmonary artery and truncus anterior. The minor fissure is divided using endosteplars, allowing the middle lobe to drop down and expose the posterior part of the artery and remaining arterial branches to the upper lobe, which are then transected.
The surgeon places the resected upper lobe into an endobag and removes it through the utility incision, completing the procedure.
